title: Conditional HTTP Fetching, Redirects, Compression, and Publisher Budgets · Runtime Contract: Feed Go Validator Precedence
summary: Every poll downloads a full feed, validators are overwritten on failure, redirects bypass scope, compressed bytes expand without limit, and retries synchronize across publishers. Predict the reader decision, feed or API contract, Go and PostgreSQL ownership, bounded failure, and observable evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the feed go validator precedence increment in publisher-friendly conditional fetch client. Learners progress through prediction,…
objectives: Store and send ETag and Last-Modified validators correctly, interpret 304 without parsing a body, and update validators only from admitted responses.
Emit field-level admitted, defaulted, dropped, conflicted, and unsupported evidence for each normalization run.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Predict Feed Go Validator Precedence · Theory Feed Go Validator Precedence
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go team handles case 5218 during an accessible community-news reader review. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove conditional HTTP, redirect and compression admission, cleanup, and publisher budgets; the release claim must bind source, migrations, dependencies, binary, image, configuration, and restored state; then change one schema, constraint, transaction, upsert, migration, query, or search plan and preserve admitted state. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Store and send ETag and Last-Modified validators correctly, interpret 304 without parsing a body, and update validators only from admitted responses.
